title: Samuel L. Jackson Has Played Basically Every Kind Of Icon — Which One Are You?
dek: From bad Bible verses to bad basketball GPAs, take this quiz and find out which Samuel L. Jackson legend lives inside you.

quiz.intro: No wrong answers — just go with your gut (and maybe channel a little righteous fury).

questions.question: It's Saturday night and your plans just got cancelled last minute. What's your move?

answers.text: Recite something ice-cold to whoever ruined my night, then walk out with my head held high.
Turn it into an emergency briefing — someone somewhere needs me right now.
Stay calm, meditate on it, and trust the plan will reveal itself.
Use the free time to plot something I've been planning for years.

questions.question: Pick a motto for your life:

answers.text: "I fix problems before they become problems."
"This weapon is my life."
"Everything happens for a reason — usually mine."
"Greater good, greater good..."

questions.question: A stranger tries to test you in public. How do you respond?

answers.text: With unmatched precision and zero wasted movement.
By quietly revealing I was three steps ahead the whole time.
By staying polite... for about ten more seconds.
By smiling and letting them dig their own grave.
Being truly, finally understood by just one person.
Balance — hero life and home life, no compromises.
Charm, laced with a healthy dose of self-interest.
Tough love, delivered loudly and without warning.

questions.question: Choose the tool that defines you best:

answers.text: Ice blasts, straight out of both wrists.
Whatever quietly gets the deal done.
A wisecrack and a very short fuse.
A whistle and a stack of signed contracts.
Cut my losses immediately. No sentimentality allowed.
Yell about it, then fix it my own way.
Enforce the consequences, no exceptions, no matter the backlash.
Take it as a sign and change my whole path in life.

questions.question: Pick your ideal role on a team:

answers.text: The reluctant hero dragged in against his will.
The mentor who demands more than anyone expects.
The one with all the best lines.
The one quietly pulling strings behind everyone's back.
Excuses. I genuinely don't want to hear them.
Disrespect. It's really that simple.
People who don't tell me things I should already know.
Impatience and sloppy technique.

questions.question: Someone doubts you'll follow through. Your reaction?

answers.text: I turn it into a whole speech about righteousness first.
I already followed through three moves ago.
I let my actions do all the talking.
I've been quietly proving it my entire life.

questions.question: Pick a genre for your own biopic:

answers.text: Spy thriller full of secrets even the audience doesn't get.
Epic space opera, tragic ending included.
Psychological thriller with a twist you never saw coming.
Family action-comedy, super-suit optional.

results.name: Jules Winnfield
results.subtitle: Pulp Fiction (1994)
results.description: You talk before you act, and when you talk, people listen — whether it's a fake Bible verse or a real threat. You're loyal to your people, allergic to disrespect, and capable of walking away from a life the second it stops making sense to you.

results.name: Nick Fury
results.subtitle: The Avengers (2012)
results.description: You're always three steps ahead of everyone else in the room, and you like it that way. You trust slowly, plan constantly, and you're the person people call when the world is actually ending.

results.name: Mace Windu
results.subtitle: Star Wars: Episode III – Revenge of the Sith (2005)
results.description: Disciplined, precise, and a little terrifying when provoked, you lead from the front and let your actions do the talking. You don't rattle easily — but when you finally do act, you go all in.

results.name: Mr. Glass (Elijah Price)
results.subtitle: Unbreakable (2000)
results.description: You're the mastermind nobody sees coming, patient enough to spend years setting something in motion. Underestimated your whole life because of how you look, you've made proving people wrong basically a personality trait.

results.name: Frozone
results.subtitle: The Incredibles (2004)
results.description: You're the reliable one who shows up exactly when it counts, balancing a secret double life without ever quite losing your cool — except when someone's hidden your suit. Family and duty matter equally to you, and you refuse to choose between them.

results.name: Ordell Robbie
results.subtitle: Jackie Brown (1997)
results.description: Smooth, charming, and always working an angle, you treat every relationship like it might be a business transaction. You're the person people underestimate right up until they realize you were three steps ahead the whole time.

results.name: Zeus Carver
results.subtitle: Die Hard with a Vengeance (1995)
results.description: You didn't ask to be the hero of this story, and you'll complain loudly about it the entire time — but you'll still show up and get it done. Reluctant, sharp-tongued, and secretly dependable, you're everyone's favorite grump.

results.name: Coach Ken Carter
results.subtitle: Coach Carter (2005)
results.description: You believe in tough love, hard rules, and holding people to a higher standard than they hold themselves. Based on the real Richmond High coach who famously locked his undefeated team out of the gym over failing grades, you'd rather be unpopular and right than liked and wrong.
